No Preview Image When You Share to LINE or Discord? Force-Refresh Social OG Cache with ?v=1

This post starts with the classic problem of sharing your blog post to a social platform and getting a stale preview image, then walks through Open Graph tags, how platforms cache og:image / og:title / og:description, why Facebook / X / LINE / Discord still show old content after you update your OG tags, and how to force a refresh by appending a cache-busting query string like `?v=1` `?v=2`. Also covers how to use the Facebook Sharing Debugger, rough cache lifetimes by platform, what to do about Twitter / X's Card Validator being retired in 2022, and whether `?v=1` impacts SEO or canonical URLs.
Don't Want to Use the Lobster (OpenClaw)? Try Claude Code Channels Instead!

Have you been using OpenClaw (the "lobster") for remote computer control? Claude Code actually has a similar feature called "Channels" that lets you push messages from Telegram or Discord into a running Claude Code Session for Claude to handle. This post walks through what Channels are, how to set them up, and real-world use cases.
Discord .well-known Domain Verification Doesn't Work on GitHub Pages — Here's the Fix

I tried to set up Discord's HTTPS domain verification on my GitHub Pages-hosted Hexo blog and hit a wall — GitHub Pages flat-out ignores .well-known directories. This post walks through what went wrong, why Hexo also needed a tweak to even generate the .well-known folder, and the workaround that actually got Discord to verify: spinning up a dedicated .well-known repo as a separate GitHub Pages site.
Common Discord Problems and How to Fix Them
A grab-bag of common Discord issues — can't log in, can't accept invites, no audio, missing DMs from strangers, and more — with fixes for each. I'll keep adding to this list as I run into new ones.
Building a Discord Chatbot with OpenAI GPT-3

This post walks through building a Discord chatbot using OpenAI GPT-3 — one that can reply to messages. Discord is actually a really good fit for integrating with OpenAI. The post focuses on GPT-3, but the sample code includes GPT-3.5 commits too.
BetterDiscord: How to Customize and Power Up Your Discord

Discord keeps blowing up — even companies are dropping Slack for it. But sometimes the default UI just isn't enough, and you want a more comfortable, customised setup. This post walks through using BetterDiscord to customise and enhance your Discord on macOS.
Discord Bots I've Used (and the Security Risks You Should Know)

I help maintain my company's Discord server, and people keep DM'ing me to ask which bots I use and how to invite Discord bots into their own servers. So here's a writeup of the bots I've used — at a high level, no deep configuration — plus a section at the end about Discord bot hacks and what to do if you're worried.
Discord Beginner's Guide: From Sign-Up to Daily Use (Complete Walkthrough)

A complete beginner's walkthrough of Discord — roles, channel icons, threads, Markdown syntax, search, and all the basics. Aimed at folks who just joined a tech community on Discord and want to find their footing.
How to Enable 2FA on Discord (Step-by-Step Guide to Protect Your Account)

How do you enable 2FA (two-factor authentication) on Discord? This step-by-step guide walks through setting it up with Google Authenticator, plus SMS authorization and backup codes so you won't get locked out if your phone dies.
Discord Notifications on Mac: Turn Them On and Tame the Server Noise

Lately a lot of services and my own company have migrated to Discord, so here's a quick note on how to enable Discord notifications on macOS and tame channel-level noise once things start flooding in.
